request.user_question: I'm travelling for work all next week, hotel gyms only and weird hours. how should I rework my training plan so I don't lose the thread?

target_response.text: Shrink the plan to two anchors and let everything else flex. Anchor one: two or three short strength sessions using whatever the hotel gym has, dumbbells or bodyweight, hitting your main movement patterns at moderate effort. Anchor two: keep easy aerobic work as walks or treadmill sessions on the days between, since travel fatigue punishes high intensity more than volume. Protect sleep hardest of all; you logged 423 minutes last night, about 7 hours, which is a decent base to defend against time-zone chaos, so fix a wake time and keep it. One honest caveat: you're logging manually, so I have no recovery, heart rate, or baseline data to tune this with, and how you feel each morning has to be the intensity dial. Log your sleep and sessions through the week and we can recalibrate the plan when you're back.
must_mention: manual logging means no recovery or heart data
